title: Set up an Astro project for content-focused sites
summary: Bootstrap an Astro project with content collections, MDX, Tailwind CSS, and SSR/static deployment — ideal for blogs, docs, and marketing sites.
declaration: I will set up an Astro project optimized for content sites (blogs, docs, landing pages). Astro ships zero JS by default — React/Vue/Svelte components are only hydrated when needed. If you want a full React app instead, consider Next.js.
instructions:
## Set up an Astro project for content-focused sites ### 1. Create the project - Run: `npm create astro@latest my-site` - When prompted by the Astro CLI: - **Template**: Choose `A blog` (includes content collections setup) or `Empty` (bare minimum) - **TypeScript**: Yes — Strict - **Install dependencies**: Yes - Change into the project directory: `cd my-site` ### 2. Understand the project structure ``` my-site/ ├── src/ │ ├── content/ # Content collections (Markdown/MDX files) │ │ ├── config.ts # Collection schemas (required) │ │ └── blog/ # A 'blog' collection │ │ └── hello-world.md │ ├── layouts/ # Shared page layouts │ ├── pages/ # File-based routing │ │ ├── index.astro # / │ │ ├── blog/ │ │ │ ├── index.astro # /blog │ │ │ └── [...slug].astro # /blog/:slug │ └── components/ # Reusable UI components ├── public/ # Static assets (images, fonts, favicon) ├── astro.config.mjs # Astro configuration └── tsconfig.json ``` ### 3. Add Tailwind CSS - Astro has an official Tailwind integration: ```bash npx astro add tailwind ``` - This command automatically installs `@astrojs/tailwind` and `tailwindcss`, and updates `astro.config.mjs`. - It also creates `tailwind.config.mjs` with the correct `content` glob for `.astro` files. - In `.astro` components, use Tailwind classes directly in the `class` attribute: ```astro

Hello World

``` ### 4. Set up Content Collections - Content Collections are Astro's type-safe system for managing Markdown/MDX content. - Define your schema in `src/content/config.ts`: ```ts import { defineCollection, z } from 'astro:content'; const blog = defineCollection({ type: 'content', // 'content' for .md/.mdx, 'data' for .json/.yaml schema: z.object({ title: z.string(), description: z.string(), pubDate: z.coerce.date(), updatedDate: z.coerce.date().optional(), heroImage: z.string().optional(), draft: z.boolean().default(false), }), }); export const collections = { blog }; ``` - Create Markdown posts in `src/content/blog/my-post.md`: ```md --- title: My First Post description: An intro post. pubDate: 2026-01-01 --- # Hello, World! This is my first blog post. ``` ### 5. Query content in pages - Use `getCollection()` to fetch and list posts: ```astro --- import { getCollection } from 'astro:content'; const posts = (await getCollection('blog')) .filter(post => !post.data.draft) .sort((a, b) => b.data.pubDate.valueOf() - a.data.pubDate.valueOf()); ---
    {posts.map(post => (
  • {post.data.title}
    • ))}
``` - Use `getEntry()` and `render()` for individual post pages: ```astro --- // src/pages/blog/[...slug].astro import { getCollection } from 'astro:content'; export async function getStaticPaths() { const posts = await getCollection('blog'); return posts.map(post => ({ params: { slug: post.slug }, props: { post }, })); } const { post } = Astro.props; const { Content } = await post.render(); --- ``` ### 6. Enable MDX (optional but recommended) - MDX lets you use React/Vue components inside your Markdown: ```bash npx astro add mdx ``` - Rename `.md` files to `.mdx` to use JSX inside them: ```mdx --- title: My MDX Post --- import MyComponent from '../../components/MyComponent.astro'; Regular **Markdown** works too. ``` ### 7. Add UI framework components (optional) - Astro's Island Architecture lets you hydrate interactive components only where needed. - Add a React island: ```bash npx astro add react ``` - Use a React component in an Astro file with a `client:*` directive: ```astro import Counter from '../components/Counter.tsx'; ``` - IMPORTANT: Components without a `client:*` directive are rendered to static HTML. JavaScript for that component is NOT shipped to the browser. ### 8. Configure SSR (server-side rendering) - By default, Astro generates fully static HTML. For dynamic pages (user sessions, API routes), enable SSR: - For Vercel: ```bash npx astro add vercel ``` - For Netlify: ```bash npx astro add netlify ``` - This updates `astro.config.mjs` to use the adapter: ```js import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config'; import vercel from '@astrojs/vercel/serverless'; export default defineConfig({ output: 'server', // 'static' | 'hybrid' | 'server' adapter: vercel(), }); ``` - `output: 'hybrid'` is the sweet spot — static by default, SSR opt-in per page with `export const prerender = false`. ### 9. Add API routes - API routes go in `src/pages/api/` with `.ts` extension: ```ts // src/pages/api/hello.ts import type { APIRoute } from 'astro'; export const GET: APIRoute = ({ request }) => { return new Response(JSON.stringify({ message: 'Hello from Astro API!' }), { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, }); }; export const POST: APIRoute = async ({ request }) => { const body = await request.json(); return new Response(JSON.stringify({ received: body })); }; ``` - NOTE: API routes only work when `output` is `'server'` or `'hybrid'`. ### 10. Build and preview - Build: `npm run build` — outputs to `./dist/` - Preview the production build locally: `npm run preview` - For static sites, deploy the `dist/` folder to any CDN (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages, GitHub Pages). ### Common errors & fixes - **`Cannot find module 'astro:content'`** — You need to run `npm run dev` or `npx astro sync` first; Astro generates `.astro/types.d.ts` automatically. - **`Collection schema validation failed`** — Your frontmatter doesn't match the Zod schema in `config.ts`. Check for missing required fields or wrong types (e.g. a string date when `z.coerce.date()` expects an ISO string). - **`getStaticPaths() required for dynamic routes`** — A page with `[slug]` in the name needs `getStaticPaths()` when `output: 'static'`. Either add the function or switch to `output: 'hybrid'` and mark the route dynamic with `export const prerender = false`. - **React component not interactive** — You added a React component but forgot the `client:*` directive. Without it, Astro renders it to HTML only. Add `client:load` to hydrate it. - **Tailwind classes not applying** — Ensure the content path in `tailwind.config.mjs` includes `'./src/**/*.{astro,html,js,ts,jsx,tsx}'`. The `npx astro add tailwind` command sets this correctly. - **Image optimization not working** — Use Astro's `` component from `astro:assets` (not a plain `` tag) to get automatic resizing and WebP conversion. - **MDX component not rendering** — MDX components must be imported in the MDX file itself, OR passed via the `components` prop of the `` component from `post.render()`. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT use `getStaticPaths()` inside a non-dynamic route file — it is only valid in files with `[param]` or `[...slug]` in the filename. - Do NOT import and use React hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`) in `.astro` files — Astro component scripts run at build time on the server; hooks are client-side only. Put interactive logic in a `.tsx` component with a `client:*` directive. - Do NOT use `document` or `window` at the top level of an `.astro` component — they are not available during SSR/build. Access them inside `
